items.title: Document per-column encoding decisions
items.avoid: A single global rule like "all strings → LabelEncoder" without inspecting cardinality or order.
items.prefer: A small table per project: column name, nominal/ordinal, nunique, chosen encoder, unseen-category policy.
items.why: Encoding is a modeling assumption; undocumented defaults cause silent mismatches when teammates extend the pipeline.
items.example:
| column | type | nunique | encoder |
| plan | nominal | 3 | one-hot |
| merchant | nominal | 40k | top-500 bucket + CV target |

items.title: Keep encoders inside CV folds
items.avoid: Fitting ColumnTransformer on full train+validation before hyperparameter search.
items.prefer: `Pipeline` passed to `cross_val_score` or `GridSearchCV` so each fold refits encoders on inner train only.
items.why: Validation folds must not influence category vocabularies or target statistics—otherwise CV scores are optimistic.

items.title: Align encoding with downstream model capabilities
items.avoid: One-hot everything for a library that supports native categoricals when cardinality is moderate.
items.prefer: Check model docs: native categorical indices for LightGBM/CatBoost; one-hot for sklearn trees and linear models on nominal data.
items.why: Unnecessary expansion wastes memory; wrong integer labels on nominal data mislead models without native support.
items.example: Pass `store_region` as pandas `category` dtype to CatBoost; use `OneHotEncoder` for the same column in sklearn `LogisticRegression`.

items.title: Plan production unseen-category behavior before launch
items.avoid: Assuming training vocabulary covers all future labels without a documented fallback.
items.prefer: `handle_unknown='ignore'`, explicit `__other__` bucket, or zero frequency—logged and monitored in serving metrics.
items.why: New SKUs, cities, and partners appear continuously; silent all-zero one-hot rows shift predictions unpredictably.
items.example: Alert when >2% of daily rows map to `__other__` for `payment_provider`—signals vocabulary drift needing retrain.
